attack_chain.description: Researcher decompiled the Cameo Android app (both the latest version and a June 2018 version) and extracted hard-coded credentials embedded in the app package.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: Motherboard decompiled both the latest version of the Android Cameo app as well as another from June 2018. The key was present in both

evidence_chunks.quote: Motherboard decompiled both the latest version of the Android Cameo app as well as another from June 2018. The key was present in both

attack_chain.description: Obtained AWS credentials hard-coded in the mobile app package that allowed access to Cameo's backend infrastructure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the Cameo app included credentials that they said allowed anyone to log into Cameo’s backend infrastructure. Specifically, the credentials granted access to Amazon S3 buckets used to store data

attack_chain.description: Authenticated to AWS backend using the harvested static credentials to verify access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The researcher said they used the credentials to access the servers “to verify what was accessible.”

evidence_chunks.quote: The researcher said they used the credentials to access the servers “to verify what was accessible.”
The researcher said they used the credentials to access the servers “to verify what was accessible.”

attack_chain.description: Accessed S3 buckets and retrieved a backup of the user database containing email addresses, hashed/salted passwords, phone numbers, messages, and non-public videos.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: I got some of the backed up user database

evidence_chunks.quote: I got some of the backed up user database
I got some of the backed up user database

initial_access_vector: Hard-coded AWS credentials embedded in the Cameo Android mobile app package, extracted via decompilation, granting access to backend S3 buckets.
persistence_mechanism: Static long-lived credentials present in the app for approximately two years (appearing in both June 2018 and current versions).
privilege_escalation: None — credentials directly granted access to backend S3 storage.
